mnemonics: FRAME: Frames Row-column Arrange Manipulate Extract
keyPoints: A data frame is a list of vectors of equal length, like a spreadsheet table.
You can subset a data frame using $, [], and [[]] for different results (vector, sub-frame, or element).
Use functions like str(), summary(), and head() to inspect a data frame's structure and contents.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that factors are stored as integers, leading to confusion when subsetting or converting.
Using matrix-style indexing [i,j] but expecting a vector result; use $ or [[ to extract a column as a vector.
Assuming all columns in a data frame are the same type; each column is a vector and can be a different type (numeric, character, factor, etc.).
